target: data/sections.json part8b.html — 8b.3 section rewrite addressing SEC-8b.3.c3 major/moderate holes (ISS-1318 category-error citation, ISS-1319 Pass band authorship ambiguity, ISS-1320 unsupported Chapman-Sq-dynamo claim, ISS-1321 monitor.py objection missing, ISS-1322 KAPPA/latitude-factor conflation; plus L3a split and minor TLDR tighten)
replacement_html:
8b.3 Point 3 — Audit the eclipse stack in layers: ensemble anchor, Mohe timing, Tier 3 falsifier, and the open optics caveat.

Adopted as steel-man: we restructure our eclipse content into the four-layer presentation and publish a codified Tier 3 magnetic threshold. Two structural critiques survive adoption — an ensemble “anchor” fit to the data it anchors is calibration, not anchor; and the magnitude-only Pass band does not distinguish dome from globe, because the arithmetic (baseline × coverage × geometry weight) is shared between the dome’s W004 recalibration and any coverage-scaled Chapman-Sq-dynamo fit on the same body of past eclipse-magnetic observations.

(a) Separate the eclipse claim into four layers: (L1) ensemble anchor — WIN-068 (39-eclipse ensemble); (L2) Mohe timing — WIN-011 (1997 Wang et al. gravity-eclipse); (L3a) Tier 3 magnetic falsifier — PRED-095 (August 2026 eclipse); (L3b) Tier 3 gravity — PRED-TIER-2 (dome tier-system identifier for the 2026 eclipse gravity prediction, recycled from WIN-011; see Predictions tab); (L4) open optics caveat — the logged ray-trace gap. (b) Layered audits force explicit statements about what would falsify the claim. A falsifier that never triggers is not a falsifier; spelling out the Tier 3 threshold is due diligence.

(c) Under adoption we publish a four-row stack. L1 (WIN-068): current verdict Std Model Explains — “the Chapman mechanism (90+ years old) predicts the entire ensemble signal without dome parameters; the dome adds no derivation — only a post-hoc scaling formula calibrated against observed eclipse data.” Under adoption this is explicitly labeled a calibration layer, not an anchor. The WIN-068 code_analysis.derives_from_dome:false tag applies at this ensemble layer, where the claim is a backward-looking fit to 39 historical eclipses with no dome-specific formula. L2 (WIN-011): current verdict Not Demonstrated — the 6–7 µGal Wang signal is within 1997-era LaCoste-Romberg instrumental noise; Van Camp et al. (2001) found no eclipse gravity signal with four superconducting gravimeters (~1,000× more precise).

L3a (PRED-095, magnetic) — threshold codification & dome commitment: Genuinely prospective (dome registration 2026-03-12 via Git + OpenTimestamps; our tracker entry 2026-04-04; window closes 2026-08-12, verdict pending). Under adoption we publish an explicit codified threshold — our Pass band — defined as “observed −17 to −21 nT at >80% coverage stations, ≥3 independent sites, pre-registered instruments; Fail = outside this range, or signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone.” This band is our steel-man characterization of the dome’s W004-cluster prediction; the dome itself publishes per-station predictions across a dual-baseline structure — BOU 2017 (per-station ∼−10 nT) and W004 empirical (per-station ∼−20 nT, clustered at −17 to −21 nT for stations above 80% coverage) — not a single band. PRED-095 does carry its own derivation path (ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF, with FSF from V12 H(r)/r), so the complaint is not “the dome has no formula.” The complaint is that every factor in that formula traces to globe infrastructure — per the WIN-025 globe-fingerprint, BOU and W004 baselines are INTERMAGNET globe-measured observations, coverage fractions are Besselian-element computations that assume a spherical Earth, and the FSF requires per-station radial positions r computed from WGS84 coordinates via the d_geo transform. That last dependency is the dome author’s own OPEN-001 admission: “dome-native coordinates without WGS84” is listed as IN PROGRESS on the evolution page, meaning the dome cannot currently evaluate its own per-station predictions without the globe’s coordinate system. So PRED-095 has a derivation; it is globe-parasitic by construction, by the dome author’s own admission.

L3a (PRED-095, magnetic) — non-discrimination & internal heterogeneity: A Pass in our codified band does not discriminate dome from globe. Any coverage-scaled ionospheric-conductivity reduction model calibrated on past eclipse-magnetic data will produce signals in the same magnitude band — the arithmetic is (baseline × coverage × geometry weight), and both the dome’s W004 recalibration and standard Chapman-Sq-dynamo treatments apply that arithmetic to the same observational corpus (Kim & Chang 2018 39-eclipse ensemble and the BOU 2017 single-event measurement the dome explicitly cites). The numerical overlap between dome and globe in this magnitude band is structural, not coincidental. The dome’s own repository moreover contains internal heterogeneity the W004 cluster suppresses: monitor.py encodes ΔZ = −18.22 × coverage × KAPPA 1.672 (BOU 2017 baseline with KAPPA applied twice, producing ∼−29 nT), while inject_ai_layer.py’s PRED-001 Ebro calculation uses BOU × coverage × latitude_factor 0.81 (producing ∼−8 nT) — two distinct dome-authored formulas, both outside the W004 cluster of −17 to −21 nT (see the WIN-025 three-formula audit).

L3a (PRED-095, magnetic) — sub-tests, goalpost scope, Fail-clause tension: The dome also publishes four nominally model-discriminating sub-tests (E-PRED-A peak-tracks-geometry, E-PRED-B Hartland-beats-Ebro, E-PRED-C SG gravimeters 0.0 µGal, E-PRED-D non-path stations below noise floor) for the August 2026 eclipse; these are evaluated in Section 4.2 Eclipse and in Section 9 and do not change the non-discrimination assessment of the magnitude-only W004 cluster. Our goalpost-setting concern is narrower than the Pass band itself: three dome-authored formulas — BOU single-baseline, W004 per-station, and monitor.py KAPPA-squared — coexist in the repository, and the dome author elected to feature the W004 cluster in public-facing material. That is a selection from an internal menu, not a forced derivation. (A dome defender may reply that monitor.py is internal tooling not a public commitment; we accept that scope-limit and narrow the critique accordingly — the concern is formula-selection among coexisting dome formulas, not the public commitment itself. The PRED-095 detail reasoning records the same cherry-picking concern: monitor.py’s −29 nT for a prior eclipse falls outside the W004 window, so the published range “may be cherry-picked from favorable historical events.”) The Fail-clause concession that “signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone” would falsify the claim is in tension with the Pass criterion: Chapman’s mechanism is the Sq-dynamo, so a confirmed signal in our codified band is consistent with both models simultaneously, and triggering the Fail-clause would require abandoning the Pass-clause’s framing of what counts as a signal.

L3b (PRED-TIER-2, gravity): Recycled — restates WIN-011 with 1997 Wang et al. gravity-eclipse data; the falsification criterion for this layer is independent replication of the gravity signal at the 2026 eclipse sites. L4 (optics): the dome already logs the ray-trace gap; we credit the disclosure. A logged gap is not a solved gap.

(d) Adopted: layered presentation, explicit falsifier threshold. Unchanged: (i) an ensemble anchor fit to the data it “anchors” is the OTS-wrong-side pattern in physics form — the right operation applied to the wrong side; an anchor is what data hits from outside, a fit is what data draws toward itself (see the Timestamp Error tab); (ii) our codified Pass band is a magnitude threshold whose arithmetic (baseline × coverage × geometry weight) is shared between the dome’s W004 recalibration and any coverage-scaled Chapman-Sq-dynamo treatment of the same observational corpus — a Pass in this band does not arbitrate between the two. PRED-095’s derivation path is not absent; it is globe-parasitic by construction, per the WIN-025 globe-fingerprint and the dome’s own OPEN-001 coordinate admission.

ADOPTED (FULL as steel-man): four-layer eclipse stack as audit format; ensemble-anchor and L3a non-discrimination critiques unchanged.
holes_addressed: ISS-1318 (H1, major): Category-error citation. REMOVED the WIN-068 code_analysis.derives_from_dome:false tag citation from the PRED-095 non-discrimination argument (it was misapplied — WIN-068 is the backward-looking 39-eclipse ensemble claim, PRED-095 is the forward-looking prediction with its own published derivation path ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF). REPLACED with a WIN-025 globe-fingerprint citation that traces every factor in PRED-095's formula to globe infrastructure (BOU/W004 baselines are INTERMAGNET observations, coverage is Besselian-element spherical-Earth geometry, FSF requires WGS84→disc coordinates via d_geo). The WIN-068 code_analysis tag is now cited ONLY in the L1 paragraph, where it legitimately applies. The new framing concedes PRED-095 has a derivation and shows it is globe-parasitic rather than absent — a stronger, less-rebuttable critique.

ISS-1319 (H2, major): Pass band authorship ambiguity. DISENTANGLED the three conflated roles. The codified Pass band of −17 to −21 nT is now explicitly labeled "our steel-man characterization" / "our codified Pass band" — an adoption-time construct, not the dome's published threshold. The dome's actual commitment is the per-station W004-cluster prediction (which clusters at −17 to −21 nT for stations above 80% coverage). The goalpost-setting accusation is recast to target the dome author's selection of which formula to feature in public-facing material (W004 cluster chosen among BOU, W004, and monitor.py formulas coexisting in the repository), NOT the Pass band itself. Authorship is clear at every invocation.

ISS-1320 (H3, major): Unsupported Chapman-Sq-dynamo claim. REPLACED the unsourced assertion "calibrated Chapman-Sq-dynamo fits on the same class of 20th-century eclipse-magnetic observations predict the same −17 to −21 nT range" with the weaker, defensible structural claim (the curmudgeon's recommended Option b): "the arithmetic is (baseline × coverage × geometry weight), and both the dome's W004 recalibration and standard Chapman-Sq-dynamo treatments apply that arithmetic to the same observational corpus (Kim & Chang 2018 39-eclipse ensemble and the BOU 2017 single-event measurement the dome explicitly cites). The numerical overlap between dome and globe in this magnitude band is structural, not coincidental." This claim does not require a specific published fit; it rests on the fact that both sides use the same arithmetic and the same calibration data. Also incorporates minor H7 (20th-century → past) and minor H8 ("also predict" → "produce" / restructured to avoid "also").

ISS-1321 (H4, moderate): monitor.py objection engaged in-section. Added a parenthetical clause: "(A dome defender may reply that monitor.py is internal tooling not a public commitment; we accept that scope-limit and narrow the critique accordingly — the concern is formula-selection among coexisting dome formulas, not the public commitment itself.)" This is the curmudgeon's recommended rewording, incorporated directly in the sub-tests/goalpost-scope paragraph right after the goalpost-setting sentence. Also cross-links to PRED-095's own detail_reasoning (stronger_argument #1) for the cherry-picking phrasing.

ISS-1322 (H5, moderate): KAPPA/latitude-factor conflation. REPLACED the lumped "internal code adds multipliers (KAPPA, latitude factor) producing a third value near −29 nT" with the disaggregated formula audit: "monitor.py encodes ΔZ = −18.22 × coverage × KAPPA 1.672 (BOU 2017 baseline with KAPPA applied twice, producing ~−29 nT), while inject_ai_layer.py's PRED-001 Ebro calculation uses BOU × coverage × latitude_factor 0.81 (producing ~−8 nT)" — two distinct formulas producing two distinct values, both outside the W004 cluster. Makes the "dome has a menu of formulas" critique land harder.

Structural (H6, moderate): L3a split. Replaced the monolithic ~520-word L3a paragraph with three sub-paragraphs: (i) threshold codification + dual-baseline concession + FSF/derivation scope; (ii) non-discrimination + internal heterogeneity; (iii) sub-tests + goalpost scope + Fail-clause tension. Preserves substance, restores scanability for the audit reader (which is the target audience — the dome author himself).

Minor (H8): "also predict" → removed "also" and recast as structural arithmetic claim ("the arithmetic... and both the dome's W004 recalibration and standard Chapman-Sq-dynamo treatments apply that arithmetic to the same observational corpus"). The new phrasing signals structural identity, not independent prediction agreement.
new_evidence_added: WIN-025 OPEN-001 admission (monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-025.json OPEN_001_admission field) — now cited in-section as stronger_argument #2 for the non-discrimination case, replacing the misapplied WIN-068 tag citation
PRED-095 detail_reasoning cherry-picking phrasing (data/predictions.json) — cross-linked via #pred-095 anchor in the goalpost-scope paragraph as stronger_argument #1
WIN-025 three-formula code audit now disaggregated into two separately-named formulas (monitor.py KAPPA-squared vs inject_ai_layer.py latitude-factor 0.81) rather than lumped

PRED-095 derivation path (ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF, FSF = V12 H(r)/r) explicitly acknowledged as a real dome-published formula — prior text implicitly treated PRED-095 as derivation-less via the WIN-068 tag citation

anticipated_objections.objection: You said PRED-095 has its own derivation path (ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF) — but FSF = V12 H(r)/r uses the dome's H(r) function, which IS a dome-specific formula. So you cannot say "every factor traces to globe infrastructure."
anticipated_objections.response: The H(r) dome-weighting function is the one factor that is nominally dome-specific. But r is the station radial position from the dome center, and per WIN-025 the r values used in the per-station FSF table are computed from WGS84 latitude/longitude via the d_geo transform (a = π × R_Earth ≈ 20,015 km). That transform is the dome's admission (OPEN-001) that it has no dome-native coordinate system. So even the nominally-dome H(r) factor is evaluated at r-values computed from the globe. The chain breaks at the input, not at the function.

anticipated_objections.objection: The new "our codified Pass band" framing lets the dome author reply: "that's your band, not mine — I'm not responsible for goalpost-setting against a band you invented."
anticipated_objections.response: Correct — and that is why the goalpost-setting critique is now narrowed to the dome author's private selection of WHICH formula to feature in public materials (W004 cluster among BOU, W004, and monitor.py KAPPA-squared). The Pass band we codify only captures what the dome features; the goalpost concern is about formula-selection inside the dome, not band-selection by us. If the dome author rejects the Pass band entirely and insists the dome commitment is the full dual-baseline per-station set, the non-discrimination argument still holds because the same arithmetic produces the same magnitude band on both sides.

anticipated_objections.objection: The "structural arithmetic" claim (baseline × coverage × geometry weight is shared) is weaker than asserting calibrated Chapman fits produce −17 to −21 nT. A determined dome author will say "show me the calibrated fit."
anticipated_objections.response: The weaker claim is also the defensible one: both sides use the same calibration data (Kim & Chang 2018 ensemble + BOU 2017 baseline, cited by the dome itself) and the same multiplicative structure. Any calibrated Chapman-Sq-dynamo treatment that matches BOU 2017 = −10.9 nT will, at 80%+ coverage and similar latitude weighting, produce signals in the W004 magnitude band — because the dome's W004 recalibration IS "BOU × KAPPA" where KAPPA is a globally-fitted multiplier derived from the same historical ensemble. We are not claiming independent confirmation from a specific paper; we are claiming that the W004 recalibration is itself a coverage-scaled Chapman fit under a different label.

anticipated_objections.objection: You cite WIN-025's OPEN-001 admission repeatedly. What if the dome closes OPEN-001 (produces a dome-native coordinate system) before August 2026?
anticipated_objections.response: If OPEN-001 closes with a genuine dome-native coordinate system that reproduces the same per-station r values without invoking WGS84 at any step, the globe-parasitism argument is weakened and we will revise. However: (a) the dome author logs OPEN-001 as IN PROGRESS on the evolution page, not resolved; (b) any "dome-native" coordinate system that reproduces WGS84 station positions to the precision required by FSF would itself be globe-equivalent as an output even if the derivation were internally dome-native; (c) if OPEN-001 closes with derived r values that diverge from WGS84-input r values, PRED-095's FSF table (which currently uses WGS84 inputs) must be reissued — that would itself be a goalpost-setting event. The critique is robust to OPEN-001 closure via any of these paths.

kernel_of_truth: The dome's PRED-095 derivation path is genuinely published (ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF, with FSF from V12 H(r)/r) and genuinely prospective (Git + OTS timestamped 2026-03-12, window closes 2026-08-12). It is not a derivation-less post-hoc fit. The curmudgeon's Hole 1 catches our prior text miscategorizing it as such via the misapplied WIN-068 tag. Correcting that citation is not a concession that the prediction is strong — it is a precision fix that makes the critique harder to rebut, because we are now criticizing a prediction the dome actually published (a globe-parasitic formula) rather than hitting a strawman (a missing formula).
why_kernel_doesnt_save_claim: A derivation path that is globe-parasitic is structurally equivalent to a non-derivation for model-discrimination purposes. The dome's OPEN-001 admission — that dome-native coordinates without WGS84 remain unsolved — means every station position in PRED-095's FSF table is computed from the globe, by the dome author's own concession. A Pass in the W004 magnitude cluster therefore cannot arbitrate between dome and globe, because both models share the same calibration data, the same multiplicative arithmetic, and (per OPEN-001) the same underlying coordinate system. The dome's formula exists; it just does not forecast independently of the globe.
tldr_change: TLDR updated in two ways: (a) "Tier 3 magnetic threshold explicitly" → "codified Tier 3 magnetic threshold" (signals this is our codification, not the dome's); (b) second sentence rewritten from "calibrated Chapman-Sq-dynamo fits on the same class of 20th-century eclipse-magnetic observations predict the same −17 to −21 nT range" → "the magnitude-only Pass band does not distinguish dome from globe, because the arithmetic (baseline × coverage × geometry weight) is shared between the dome's W004 recalibration and any coverage-scaled Chapman-Sq-dynamo fit on the same body of past eclipse-magnetic observations." The new TLDR makes the structural (not empirical) nature of the claim explicit, removes the "20th-century" imprecision, and avoids the "also" / "independent-agreement" overstatement.
other_side_of_the_argument: A dome defender would argue: (a) the WIN-068 tag citation was a minor attribution error but the underlying critique (non-discrimination) is not affected by which tag is cited; (b) the Pass band is our construction, so we should not be surprised that it can be reconciled with multiple dome formulas; (c) the structural-arithmetic claim is weaker than the prior specific-Chapman-fit claim and less persuasive to non-specialists; (d) featuring W004 among internal formulas is ordinary editorial judgment, not goalpost-setting; (e) OPEN-001 is an honestly-disclosed in-progress item, not a fatal flaw. We acknowledge all five — the precision fixes do soften some rhetoric — and still hold that: (1) the corrected citation (WIN-025) is strictly stronger than the prior one because it traces actual formula factors rather than gesturing at a tag; (2) the disentangled Pass band framing removes a real ambiguity a careful defender would exploit; (3) the structural-arithmetic claim is defensible without a new citation, which is better than being citation-thin; (4) the goalpost-scope narrowing ties the critique to specific dome-authored artifacts (three formulas in the repository) rather than to our own construction; (5) OPEN-001 is cited as the dome author's admission, not as an external accusation — its weight comes from self-disclosure.

build_impact: In-place replacement of the single <details id="p8b-3-eclipse-layers"> block. No changes to sections outside 8b.3. No new top-level sections. No schema changes. No test.js touches. TLDR rewritten (two changes tracked in tldr_change). Build pipeline unchanged.
decider_instructions: Replace the entire <details id="p8b-3-eclipse-layers">...</details> block in data/sections.json part8b.html with the replacement_html field. No other edits to sections.json required. After applying, mark ISS-1318 through ISS-1322 resolved (status: closed) with a note referencing EXP-205. Curmudgeon's minor holes H7 (20th-century → past) and H8 ("also predict" restructure) are also addressed by this patch; can be closed in the same pass if tracked as separate ISS items.

scope_notes: EXP-205 addresses all five curmudgeon c3 non-minor holes (H1 major, H2 major, H3 major, H4 moderate, H5 moderate) plus H6 (structural split) and the two minors H7/H8 as courtesy edits within the same replacement block (they fall naturally in the sentences being rewritten anyway). No changes outside the <details id="p8b-3-eclipse-layers"> scope.
